chapter_title.ar: بَابُ حُبِّ الدُّنْيَا وَ الْحِرْصِ عَلَيْهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 126 – Love of the world and the greed upon it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/228)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/228)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَا ذِئْبَانِ ضَارِيَانِ فِي غَنَمٍ قَدْ فَارَقَهَا رِعَاؤُهَا أَحَدُهُمَا فِي أَوَّلِهَا وَ الْآخَرُ فِي آخِرِهَا بِأَفْسَدَ فِيهَا مِنْ حُبِّ الْمَالِ وَ الشَّرَفِ فِي دِينِ الْمُسْلِمِ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Hammad Bin Bashir who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘Two ferocious wolves among sheep who have separated from their shepherd, one of the two being among its front ones and the other among its back ones, are no more damaging among them than the love of the wealth and the honour (popularity) is in the Religion of the Muslim’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali has narrated from his father from ibn Faddal from ibn Bukayr from Hammad ibn Bashir who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) saying, ‘No two fierce wolves in a flock of sheep in the absence of their shepherd, one wolf on either side is as destructive to them as the love of property and honor is to the religion of a Muslim.”
